description:
You have just joined the “Binary-Curation” team.  
    Your first job is to prepare a reproducible Python environment that the artifact-manager can later use to mirror a single, vetted wheel.

    1. Create a Python virtual environment that lives at  
       /home/user/.venvs/curation_env  
       (The directory /home/user/.venvs does not yet exist.)

    2. Inside that virtual environment, install exactly version 1.26.15 of the package “urllib3” **without its dependencies**.  
       Hint: pip has a flag that skips dependency resolution.

    3. Generate a lock-file called  
       /home/user/curation/output/locked_requirements.txt  
       containing one and only one line:  
       urllib3==1.26.15  
       • No extra blank lines.  
       • No leading or trailing spaces.

    4. Produce a tiny status report for the artifact-manager at  
       /home/user/curation/output/curation_report.log  
       The file must contain exactly two lines, in this order:

       ENV_PATH=/home/user/.venvs/curation_env  
       LOCK_FILE=/home/user/curation/output/locked_requirements.txt  

       • Use literal uppercase key names shown above.  
       • Use an equals sign (=) with no surrounding spaces.  
       • No extra lines before, after, or between.

Important:  
• The directory /home/user/curation/output must be created by you with the correct parent directories.  
• All paths are absolute; do not place files elsewhere.  
• After running your commands, someone should be able to:

      source /home/user/.venvs/curation_env/bin/activate
      pip install -r /home/user/curation/output/locked_requirements.txt

  and obtain urllib3 1.26.15 only.

The automated tests will:
• Verify that /home/user/.venvs/curation_env exists and is a valid virtual environment.  
• Parse locked_requirements.txt to confirm it contains exactly the single required line.  
• Read curation_report.log and check that both key/value lines appear exactly as specified, in the correct order and without extra whitespace.

Do not show the test commands; simply perform the steps in the terminal until everything matches the required state.
truth:
Expected artifacts after the agent finishes:

    1. Directory structure (all relative to /home/user):
       .venvs/curation_env/           (standard venv layout)
       curation/output/locked_requirements.txt
       curation/output/curation_report.log

    2. /home/user/curation/output/locked_requirements.txt  
       EXACT content (single terminating newline allowed):
       ─────────────────────────────────
       urllib3==1.26.15
       ─────────────────────────────────

    3. /home/user/curation/output/curation_report.log  
       EXACT content (single terminating newline allowed):
       ─────────────────────────────────
       ENV_PATH=/home/user/.venvs/curation_env
       LOCK_FILE=/home/user/curation/output/locked_requirements.txt
       ─────────────────────────────────
